bitty documentation

Documentation

data-* attributes

Overview

  • The HTML side of the bitty API consists of these four attributes.
  • Attributes can be added to HTML elements to send and receive signals through JavaScript methods.

Items

data-connect
Description
  • bitty automatically looks for any classes on the window under `window.BittyClasses = {}`.
  • Adding a `data-connect` attribute with the path to a modules will classes from the external file as well.
Examples
Connect to the default class from a remote module. [0030-connection-to-default-module-class]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 
  data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/data-connect-single-class.js"
></bitty-8-0>
<button data-send="signal_15881">Test</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_15881">waiting</div>
JavaScript
// this content is in an external file
// TODO: show the external file here.
Test Trigger
// This example is triggered from
// the remote file.
Results
waiting
data-listeners
Examples
Listen for specific event on elements. [0005-custom-listner]
HTML
<div data-listeners="mouseover" data-send="signal_CF88C">
  Manual mouseover target CF88C
</div>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_CF88C">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_CF88C(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}
Test Trigger
// This test is triggered manually by
// mousing over the text.
Results
Manual mouseover target CF88C
waiting
Notes
  • This example test requires manually mousing over the target text for it to trigger
Only send signals when the specified listener is fired (i.e. don't respond to `click` or `input` unless they are included in the `data-listeners` value). [0020-only-send-for-specified-events]
HTML
<button
  data-listeners="customeventC23DC" 
  data-send="signal_C23DC">
  Test C23DC
</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_C23DC">test passed</div>
JavaScript
signal_C23DC(_, el) {
  // This does not fire because a click
  // event is not one of the listeners.
  el.innerHTML = "test failed ";
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  await this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_C23DC]").click();
}
Results
test passed
Adding a default event (e.g. `click`) to the `data-listeners` attribute makes it active again. [0037-add-default-to-globals]
HTML
<button data-listeners="customevent22223 click" data-send="signal_22223">
  Test 22223
</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_22223">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_22223(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_22223]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Adding a `data-listeners` attribute only affects the specific element (e.g. `this.trigger()` will send signals that activate regardless of if there's an element with a `data-listeners` attribute that has a value with the same name). [0060-tigger-overrides-listener]
HTML
<div data-listeners="listenerthatisnotused" data-send="signal_0690F"></div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_0690F">bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_0690F(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.trigger("signal_0690F");
}
Results
bug
Notes
  • bitty always listens for `click` and `input` events. If you need something to not respond to those events add a `data-listeners` attribute with only the listeners you want to use for the element.
  • The `data-listeners` attributes only effect sending elements (i.e. elements with `data-send` attributes on them).
  • Adding a `data-listeners` to the `<>` tag itself does nothing.
  • The specific set of custom listeners is defined based on elements that exist with a `data-listeners` attribute when the module loads. That is, if a new element is added after a bitty module has initialized with a new listener type in a `data-listener` attribute value it won't be picked up.
data-receive
Examples
Receive a single signal. [0010-single-data-receive]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_90DD9">Test 90DD9</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_90DD9">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_90DD9(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}
Test Trigger
bittyReady() {
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_90DD9]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Receive multiple signals. [0020-receive-multiple-signals]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_CBE64">Test CBE64-1</button>
<button data-send="signal_CBE64_2">Test CBE64-2</button>
<button data-send="signal_CBE64_3">Test CBE64-3</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_CBE64 signal_CBE64_2 signal_CBE64_3">
  waiting
</div>
JavaScript
signal_CBE64(_, el) {
  this.message = ["test"];
  el.innerHTML = "caught button 1";
}

signal_CBE64_2(_, el) {
  this.message.push("passed");
  el.innerHTML = "caught button 2";
}

signal_CBE64_3(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = this.message.join(" ");
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.trigger(`signal_CBE64`);
  await this.sleep(50);
  this.trigger(`signal_CBE64_2`);
  await this.sleep(50);
  this.trigger(`signal_CBE64_3`);
}
Results
waiting
Multiple elements receive the same signal. [0030-multiple-receivers]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_327E5">Test 327E5</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_327E5">waiting</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_327E5">waiting</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_327E5">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_327E5(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}
Test Trigger
bittyReady() {
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_327E5").click();
}
Results
waiting
waiting
waiting
Notes
  • Every receiving element is passed through the method independently.
  • Processing the individual receiving elements can happen in any order.
Signal methods can be async. [0040-async-methods]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_F9422">Test F9422</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_F9422">bug</div>
JavaScript
async signal_F9422(_, el) {
  const t1 = performance.now();
  await this.sleep(100);
  const t2 = performance.now();
  const delta = t2 - t1;
  if (delta > 80) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
bittyReady() {
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_F9422]").click();
}
Results
bug
data-send
Examples
Send a single signal from a click event. [0010-basic-send]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_ABF58">Test</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_ABF58">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_ABF58(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  await this.sleep(100);
  document.querySelector("[data-send~=signal_ABF58]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Send a single signal that's picked up by multiple receivers. [0020-single-to-multiple]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_16851">Test</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_16851">waiting</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_16851">waiting</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_16851">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_16851(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  await this.sleep(100);
  document.querySelector("[data-send~=signal_16851]").click();
}
Results
waiting
waiting
waiting
Send multiple signals at the same time. [0030-send-multiple-signals]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_148C2 signal_148C2_2 signal_148C2_3">
  Test
</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_148C2">waiting</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_148C2_2">waiting</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_148C2_3">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_148C2(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}

signal_148C2_2(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}

signal_148C2_3(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  await this.sleep(100);
  document.querySelector("[data-send~=signal_148C2]").click();
}
Results
waiting
waiting
waiting
Notes
  • Each signal is fired async.
  • Signals might be processed in any order (i.e. given `data-send="alfa bravo"` there is no guarantee that `alfa` will run before `bravo`.
  • If you need to ensure methods run in a particular order then trigger only one event from bitty and then use `this.trigger()` or `this.send()` in your methods to chain to the next one.
Signals can be sent without corresponding `data-receive="SIGNALS"` elements. When that happens, they fire only once. [0060-no-receive-required]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_0773E">Test</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_0773E">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_0773E(_, __) {
  this.trigger("verify_signal_0773E");
}

verify_signal_0773E(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  await this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_0773E]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Events bubble to parent senders. (e.g. if you click a button that doesn't have a `data-send` attribute it bubbles up to ancestors that do have one and the signal is sent from there. TODO: Introduce concept of sender and targets) [0070-events-bubble-to-ancestor-senders]
HTML
<div data-send="signal_05308">
  <button id="el-05308">
    Test Button
  </button>
</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_05308">bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_05308(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  await this.sleep(100);
  document.querySelector("[data-send=signal_05308]").click();
}
Results
bug
Events are sent for every ancestor of the target. [0075-send-for-ancestor]
HTML
<div data-send="signal_E3ABB_3">
  <div data-send="signal_E3ABB_2">
    <button data-send="signal_E3ABB">Test</button>
  </div>
</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_E3ABB">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_E3ABB_2">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_E3ABB_3">bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_E3ABB(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}

signal_E3ABB_2(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}

signal_E3ABB_3(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  await this.sleep(100);
  document.querySelector("[data-send=signal_E3ABB]").click();
}
Results
bug
bug
bug

bittyReady()

Overview

Items

bit Methods and Properties

Overview

Items

addListener(type) [Done]
Description
  • `this.addListener(type, signals)` adds listeners for events that don't have elements to attach to.
Examples
Listen for events that aren't attached to elements. [0010-add-listeners]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_1D1AB">waiting</div>
JavaScript
async bittyReady() {
  this.addSignalListener("customevent1D1AB", "signal_1D1AB");
  this.sleep(100);
  const targetEvent = new CustomEvent1D1AB();
  dispatchEvent(targetEvent);
}

signal_1D1AB(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}
Extra HTML
<script>
class CustomEvent1D1AB extends Event {
  constructor() {
    super("customevent1D1AB", { bubbles: true });
  }
}
</script>
Test Trigger
Results
waiting
bitClass [Done]
Description
  • The name of the Class that defined the bit.
Examples
Get the name of the class that defined the bit. [0010-return-bit-class-name]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_E0BA2">Test E0BA2</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_E0BA2">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_E0BA2(_, el) {
  if (this.bitClass === "ClassE0BA2"){ 
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_E0BA2]").click();
}
Results
waiting
copy(selector) [Done]
Description
  • TODO: Add error logging
  • TODO: Defined what happens if multiple items match the selector.
Examples
Copy text from the value of the selected element. [0010-copy-from-value]
HTML
<p>
  NOTE: This example is run manually because it
  uses the operating systems copy/paste system.
  Use the button to copy the text. Paste 
  it into another document to confirm it
  worked. 
</p>
<textarea class="el-B768A">text from textarea id-B768A</textarea>
<button class="manual-test" data-send="signal_B768A">Copy Text id-B768A</button>
JavaScript
async signal_B768A(_, __) {
  await this.copy(".el-B768A");
}
Test Trigger
Results

NOTE: This example is run manually because it uses the operating systems copy/paste system. Use the button to copy the text. Paste it into another document to confirm it worked.

Copy innerHTML if there is no value on the selected element. [0020-copy-inner]
HTML
<p>
  NOTE: This example is run manually because it
  uses the operating systems copy/paste system.
  Use the button to copy the text. Paste 
  it into another document to confirm it
  worked. 
</p>
<div class="el-3611F">text from div id-3611F</div>
<button class="manual-test" data-send="signal_3611F">Test Manually</button>
JavaScript
async signal_3611F(_, __) {
  await this.copy(".el-3611F");
}
Test Trigger
Results

NOTE: This example is run manually because it uses the operating systems copy/paste system. Use the button to copy the text. Paste it into another document to confirm it worked.

text from div id-3611F
createElement(key, content) [Done]
Description
  • Adds a element to the `this.element[key]` collection
Examples
Add from a string. [0010-from-string]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_2B6BB">Test 2B6BB</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_2B6BB">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_2B6BB(_, el) {
  const elementString = `<div class="test">test passed</div>`;
  this.createElement("el_2B6BB", elementString);
  el.replaceWith(this.renderElement("el_2B6BB"));
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_2B6BB]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Creating an element returns a result object with an `ok` value of true if the element was added successfully. [0015-ok-true]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_EE7C0">Test EE7C0</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_EE7C0">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_EE7C0(_, el) {
  const elementString = `<div></div>`;
  const result = this.createElement("el_EE7C0", elementString);
  if (result.ok === true) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_EE7C0]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Creating an element returns a result object with an `ok` value of `false` if the element could not be added. [0018-ok-false]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_2C3F3">Test 2C3F3</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_2C3F3">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_2C3F3(_, el) {
  this.setGlobalLogLevel("none");
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  const result = this.createElement("el_2C3F3", {
    misc: "invalid payload",
  });
  if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_2C3F3]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Use an existing element. [0020-from-element]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_C54A9">Test C54A9</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_C54A9">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_C54A9(_, el) {
  const newEl = document.createElement("div");
  newEl.classList.add("test");
  newEl.innerHTML = "test passed";
  this.createElement("el_C54A9", newEl);
  el.replaceWith(this.renderElement("el_C54A9"));
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_C54A9]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Use a document fragment (in which case the first child of the fragment is used. Any other elements are ignored.) [0030-from-fragment]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_DA138">Test DA138</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_DA138">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_DA138(_, el) {
  const template = document.createElement("template");
  template.innerHTML = `<div class="test">test passed</div>`;
  this.createElement("el_DA138", template.content);
  el.replaceWith(this.renderElement("el_DA138"));
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_DA138]").click();
}
Results
waiting
If there is more than one element in a document fragment only the first one is used. A warning is logged and returned. [0035-warn-on-extra-fragment-elements]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_4C991">Test 4C991</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_4C991">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_4C991(_, el) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.setGlobalLogLevel("none");
  const template = document.createElement("template");
  template.innerHTML = `<div></div><div></div>`;
  const result = this.createElement("el_4C991", template.content);
  if (result.level === "warn" && result.ok === true) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_4C991]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Elements are added to storage automatically. [0040-store-elements]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_6CF16">Test 6CF16</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_6CF16">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_6CF16(_, el) {
  this.createElement("el_6CF16", `<div class="test">test passed</div>`);
  delete this._element["el_6CF16"];
  this.loadElement("el_6CF16");
  el.replaceWith(this.renderElement("el_6CF16"));
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_6CF16]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Adding an element with the same key as an existing item overwrites the older one. The returned result is a `warn` with a message about the overwrite. The `result.ok` value is still `true`. [0050-new-overwrite-old]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_374D9">Test 374D9</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_374D9">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_374D9(_, el) {
  this.setGlobalLogLevel("none");
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createElement("el_374D9", "<div></div>");
  const result = this.createElement("el_374D9", "<div></div>");
  if (result.level === "warn" && result.ok === true) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_374D9]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Log and return an error if no value was passed in with which to make the element. [0060-error-if-no-value]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_73040">Test 73040</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_73040">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_73040(_, el) {
  this.setGlobalLogLevel("none");
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  const result = this.createElement("el_73040");
  if (result.level === "error") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_73040]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Log and return an error result if something other than a String, Element, or Document Fragment was passed to the method. [0070-error-invalid-input]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_C244B">Test C244B</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_C244B">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_C244B(_, el) {
  const result = this.createElement("el_C244B", { misc: "not a valid input" });
  if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_C244B]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Notes
  • TODO: Display parsing HTML Footnote
createFragment(key, content) [Done]
Examples
Add from a string [0010-add-from-string]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_2C4FE">Test 2C4FE</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_2C4FE">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_2C4FE(_, el) {
  this.createFragment("el_2C4FE", `<div>x</div><div>test passed</div>`);
  el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment("el_2C4FE").children[1].innerHTML;
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_2C4FE]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Log and provide success result. [0020-ok-true-on-success]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_D2EBD">Test D2EBD</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_D2EBD">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_D2EBD(_, el) {
  const result = this.createFragment("el_D2EBD", `<div>x</div><div>ok</div>`);
  if (result.ok === true && result.level === "info") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_D2EBD]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Error on fail [0030-ok-false-on-failure]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_C8DB8">Test C8DB8</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_C8DB8">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_C8DB8(_, el) {
  const result = this.createFragment("el_C8DB8", {
    key: "not a string, element, or document fragment",
  });
  if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_C8DB8]").click();
}
Results
waiting
From fragment. [0040-from-fragment]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_A0EAB">Test A0EAB</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_A0EAB">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_A0EAB(_, el) {
  const fragment = document.createDocumentFragment();
  const element = document.createElement("div");
  element.innerHTML = "test passed";
  fragment.appendChild(element);
  this.createFragment("el_A0EAB", fragment);
  el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment("el_A0EAB").firstChild.innerHTML;
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_A0EAB]").click();
}
Results
waiting
From element. [0050-from-element]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_69369">Test 69369</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_69369">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_69369(_, el) {
  const element = document.createElement("div");
  element.innerHTML = "test passed";
  this.createFragment("el_69369", element);
  el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment("el_69369").firstChild.innerHTML;
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_69369]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Store automatically. [0060-store-automatically]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_C87DB">Test C87DB</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_C87DB">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_C87DB(_, el) {
  this.deleteFragment("el_C87DB");
  this.createFragment("el_C87DB", `<div class="test">test passed</div>`);
  delete this._fragment["el_C87DB"];
  const result = this.loadFragment("el_C87DB");
  el.replaceWith(this.renderFragment("el_C87DB"));
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_C87DB]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Warn if overwriting existing key. [0070-warn-on-overwrite]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_3D5CF">Test 3D5CF</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_3D5CF">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_3D5CF(_, el) {
  this.setGlobalLogLevel("none");
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createFragment("el_3D5CF", `<div></div>`);
  const result = this.createFragment("el_3D5CF", `<div></div>`);
  if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_3D5CF]").click();
}
Results
waiting
No value returns error. [0080-no-value-returns-error]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_2F03D">Test 2F03D</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_2F03D">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_2F03D(_, el) {
  this.setGlobalLogLevel("none");
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  const result = this.createFragment("el_2F03D");
  if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_2F03D]").click();
}
Results
waiting
No key and no fallback returns error. [0090-no-key-no-value-returns-error]

<button data-send="signal_FE03B">Test FE03B</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_FE03B">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_FE03B(_, el) {
  this.setGlobalLogLevel("none");
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  const result = this.createFragment();
  if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_FE03B]").click();
}
Results
waiting
createJSON(key, data) [Done]
Description
  • Adds a new entry with `key` to `this.json[key]`.
Examples
Create a new `this.json[key]` entry from a string. [0010-add-from-string]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_390C9">Test 390C9</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_390C9">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_390C9(_, el) {
   const jsonString = `{ "status": "test passed" }`;
   this.createJSON("el_390C9", jsonString);
   el.innerHTML = this.json.el_390C9.status;
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_390C9]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Create a new `this.json[key]` entry from a JSON object. [0020-json-object-add]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_6A436">Test 6A436</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_6A436">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_6A436(_, el) {
  const jsonObject = JSON.parse(`{ "status": "test passed" }`);
  this.createJSON("el_6A436", jsonObject);
  el.innerHTML = this.json["el_6A436"].status;
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_6A436]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Create a new `this.json[key]` entry from a JavaScript Object. [0022-objects-added]

Store new JSONs. [0040-store-jsons]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_41D2B">Test 41D2B</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_41D2B">waiting</div>
JavaScript
#key = "json_signal_41D2B";

signal_41D2B(_, el) {
  // this.loadJSON("el_41D2B");
  // el.innerHTML = this.json["el_41D2B"].status;
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setGlobalLogLevel("none");
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.deleteJSON("el_41D2B");
  this.createJSON("el_41D2B", { status: "ok" });
  // delete this.json["el_41D2B"];
  // this.trigger("signal_41D2B");
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_41D2B]").click();
}

waiting
JavaScript objects are turned into JSON objects which removes any methods they have. [0050-objects-stringified]

waiting
Adding a JSON with an existing `key` overwrites the earlier version (the return value is a "warn") [0080-overwrite-existing]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_B76EB">Test B76EB</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_B76EB">waiting</div>
JavaScript
#key = "json_signal_B76EB";

signal_B76EB(_, el) {
  // const result = this.createJSON("el_B76EB", { status: "ok" });
  // if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
  //   el.innerHTML = this.json["el_B76EB"].status;
  // }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.deleteJSON("el_B76EB");
  this.createJSON("el_B76EB", { status: "bug" });
  this.trigger("signal_B76EB");
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_B76EB]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Log and return an error if no argument for the JSON was passed in. [0090-error-on-nothing-passed-in]

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_45CAA">waiting</div>
JavaScript
#key = "json_signal_45CAA";

signal_45CAA(_, el) {
  const result = this.createJSON("el_45CAA");
  if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.trigger("signal_45CAA");
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_45CAA]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Notes
  • Items stored in localStorage are moved under a top level `data` key in the object and stringified. (e.g. `{ "example": "alfa" }` becomes the string `{ "data": { "example": "alfa" } }` in localStorage.
  • The related bit methods work with the same top level `data` key in local storage. Those methods being:
    • `.fetchJSON(url [, options = {}])`
    • `.loadJSON(key, fallback)`
    • `.deleteJSON(key)`
    • `.saveJSON(key)`
createSVG(key, content) [Done]
Examples
Add from a string [0010-add-from-string]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_58C94">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_58C94"></div>
JavaScript
#key = "svg_signal_58C94";

signal_58C94(input, el) {
  this.createSVG("el_58C94", input);
  const svg = this.renderSVG("el_58C94");
  el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
  this.send(svg, "view_signal_58C94");
}

view_signal_58C94(svg, el) {
  el.replaceWith(svg);
}


bittyReady() {
  const input = `
    <svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
      <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
      <text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
    </svg>`;
  this.send(input, "signal_58C94");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
From another SVG. [0040-from-svg]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_5112B">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_5112B"></div>
JavaScript
#key = "svg_signal_5112B";

signal_5112B(input, el) {
  this.createSVG("el_5112B", input);
  const svg = this.renderSVG("el_5112B");
  el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
  this.send(svg, "view_signal_5112B");
}

view_signal_5112B(svg, el) {
  el.replaceWith(svg);
}


bittyReady() {
  const input = document.createElementNS("http://www.w3.org/2000/svg", "svg");
  input.setAttribute("width", "60");
  input.setAttribute("height", "40");
  input.innerHTML = `<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
<text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>`;
  this.send(input, "signal_5112B");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Store automatically. [0060-store-automatically]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_D4DA1">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_D4DA1"></div>
JavaScript
#key = "svg_signal_D4DA1";

signal_D4DA1(_, el) {
  // this.loadSVG("el_D4DA1");
  // const svg = this.renderSVG("el_D4DA1");
  // el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
  // this.send(svg, "view_signal_D4DA1");
}

view_signal_D4DA1(svg, el) {
  el.replaceWith(svg);
}


bittyReady() {
  const input = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
<text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>`;
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.deleteSVG("el_D4DA1");
  this.createSVG("el_D4DA1", input);
  delete this._svg["el_D4DA1"];
  this.trigger("signal_D4DA1");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Warn if overwriting existing key. [0070-warn-on-overwrite]

  }

svg>`;

debug(message)

deleteElement(key) [Done]
Examples
Remove and existing element. [0010-remove-existing]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_E84D4">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_E84D4";

signal_E84D4(_, el) {
  this.deleteElement("el_E84D4");
  if (this.renderElement("el_E84D4") === undefined) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createElement("el_E84D4", `<div>ok</div>`);
  this.trigger("signal_E84D4");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Elements are removed from storage as well. [0020-storage-removal]

signal_DE329(_, el) {

  const result = this.loadElement("el_DE329");
  if (result.level === "error" && result.ok === false) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createElement("el_DE329", `<div>ok</div>`);
  this.trigger("signal_DE329");
}
Test Trigger

deleteFragment(key) [Done]
Description
  • Deletes a fragment with the given key from the collection of fragments.
  • Deletes it from storage as well
Examples
Delete an existing fragment from the collection. [0010-remove-existing]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_108CD">bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_108CD(_, el) {
  this.deleteFragment("el_108CD");
  if (this._fragment["el_108CD"] === undefined) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_108CD");
}

given_signal_108CD(_, __) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createFragment("el_108CD", "<div></div>");
  this.trigger("signal_108CD");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Fragments are deleted from storage as well. [0020-storage-removal]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_E279E">bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_E279E(_, el) {
  this.deleteFragment("el_E279E");
  const result = this.loadFragment("el_E279E");
  if (result.ok === false) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_E279E");
}

given_signal_E279E(_, __) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createFragment("el_E279E", "<div></div>");
  this.trigger("signal_E279E");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug

deleteJSON(key) [Done]
Description
  • Removes `this.json[key]`.
Examples
Use `this.deleteJSON(key)` to remove an existing `this.json[key]` item. [0010-removing-existing-json]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_69E87">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "json_signal_69E87";

signal_69E87(_, el) {
  this.deleteJSON("el_69E87");
  if (this.json["el_69E87"] === undefined) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createJSON("el_69E87", `{}`);
  this.trigger("signal_69E87");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug

    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }

vg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
<text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
svg>`;
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createSVG("el_CB6CF", input);

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_1D251">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "svg_signal_1D251";

signal_1D251(_, el) {
  this.deleteSVG("el_1D251");
  const result = this.loadSVG("el_1D251");
  if (result.level === "error" && result.ok === false) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  const input = `
vg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
<text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
svg>`;
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createSVG("el_1D251", input);
  this.trigger("signal_1D251");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Log and return `ok === true` if the item is removed. [0030-return-ture]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_35ED0">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "svg_signal_35ED0";

signal_35ED0(_, el) {
  const result = this.deleteSVG("el_35ED0");
  if (result.ok === true) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  const input = `
vg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
<text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
svg>`;
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createSVG("el_35ED0", input);
  this.trigger("signal_35ED0");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Log and return a warning with `ok === true` if the requested key for removal doesn't exist. [0040-warn-for-missing]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_A473A">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "svg_signal_A473A";

signal_A473A(_, el) {
  const result = this.deleteSVG("el_A473A");
  if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.deleteSVG("el_A473A");
  this.trigger("signal_A473A");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
fetchElement(key, url, fallback = null, options = {}) [Done]
Description
  • Fetch HTML from a URL and store it as an element in `this.element[key]`.
Examples
Fetch an element from a URL. [0010-basic-fetch]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_49058">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_49058";

async signal_49058(_, el) {
  const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-element.xml";
  await this.fetchElement("el_49058", url);
  el.replaceWith(this.renderElement("el_49058"));
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("signal_49058");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
`fetchElement()` has a return value object with `.ok === true` when the fetch succeeds. [0030-return-ok]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_0938C">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_0938C";

async signal_0938C(_, el) {
  const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-element.xml";
  const result = await this.fetchElement("el_0938C", url);
  if (result.ok === true) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("signal_0938C");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
If the incoming data is a Document Fragment only the first element is used. A warn message is logged and returned as the result. [0032-fragments-truncated]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_E0510">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_E0510";

async signal_E0510(_, el) {
  const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-fragment.xml";
  const result = await this.fetchElement("el_E0510", url);
  if (
    result.level === "warn"
  ) {
    el.replaceWith(this.renderElement("el_E0510"));
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.trigger("signal_E0510");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Fetched elements are added to storage. [0035-add-local-storage]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_92749">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_92749";

async signal_92749(_, el) {
  // this.loadElement("el_92749");
  // el.replaceWith(this.renderElement("el_92749"));
}


async bittyReady() {
  // const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-element.xml";
  // await this.fetchElement("el_92749", url);
  // delete this._element["el_92749"];
  // this.trigger("signal_92749");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Return a result with `level === "error"` and `ok === false` if there's an issue with the fetch. [0040-fetch-issue]
HTML
<p>NOTE: This test causes error messages to show up in 
  the console. The test is run manually to prevent
  the noise from showing up on initial page load.</p>
<button data-send="signal_EFEFA">Test Manually</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_EFEFA">waiting</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_EFEFA";

async signal_EFEFA(_, el) {
  const url = "/intentionally-missing-file.html";
  const result = await this.fetchElement("el_EFEFA", url);
  if (result.level === "error" && result.ok === false) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results

NOTE: This test causes error messages to show up in the console. The test is run manually to prevent the noise from showing up on initial page load.

waiting
Fallback to element from string if fetch fails. [0050-fallback-string]
HTML
<p>NOTE: This tests causes expected errors in the console. 
  It's run manually to avoid noise on the initial page load
</p>
<button data-send="run_signal_BB20D">Test Manually</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_BB20D">waiting</div>
JavaScript
#key = "element_signal_BB20D";

async signal_BB20D(payload, el) {
  await this.fetchElement("el_BB20D", payload.url, payload.fallback);
  el.replaceWith(this.renderElement("el_BB20D"));
}


run_signal_BB20D(_, __) {
  const url = "/intentionally-missing-file.html";
  const fallback = `<div class="manual-test">ok</div>`;
  this.send(
    { url: url, fallback: fallback },
    "signal_BB20D",
  );
}
Test Trigger
Results

NOTE: This tests causes expected errors in the console. It's run manually to avoid noise on the initial page load

waiting
Fallback to an element if fetch fails. [0053-fallback-element]
HTML
<p>NOTE: This tests causes expected errors in the console. 
  It's run manually to avoid noise on the initial page load
</p>
<button data-send="run_signal_A83DA">Test Manually</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_A83DA">waiting</div>
JavaScript
#key = "element_signal_A83DA";

async signal_A83DA(payload, el) {
  await this.fetchElement("el_A83DA", payload.url, payload.fallback);
  el.replaceWith(this.renderElement("el_A83DA"));
}


run_signal_A83DA(_, __) {
  const url = "/intentionally-missing-file.html";
  const fallback = document.createElement("div");
  fallback.innerHTML = "test passed";
  fallback.classList.add("manual-test");
  this.send(
    { url: url, fallback: fallback },
    "signal_A83DA",
  );
}
Test Trigger
Results

NOTE: This tests causes expected errors in the console. It's run manually to avoid noise on the initial page load

waiting
Fallback to a document fragment if the fetch fails. (If there is more than one element at the root of the fragment then the first one becomes the element. The rest are dropped.) [0055-fragment-fallback]
HTML
<p>NOTE: This tests causes expected errors in the console. 
  It's run manually to avoid noise on the initial page load
</p>
<button data-send="run_signal_F5B52">Test Manually</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_F5B52">waiting</div>
JavaScript
#key = "element_signal_F5B52";

async signal_F5B52(payload, el) {
  await this.fetchElement("el_F5B52", payload.url, payload.fallback);
  el.replaceWith(this.renderElement("el_F5B52"));
}


run_signal_F5B52(_, __) {
  const url = "/intentionally-missing-file.html";
  const fallback = document.createElement("template");
  fallback.innerHTML = `<div class="manual-test">ok</div>`;
  this.send(
    { url: url, fallback: fallback.content },
    "signal_F5B52",
  );
}
Test Trigger
Results

NOTE: This tests causes expected errors in the console. It's run manually to avoid noise on the initial page load

waiting
If a fetch overwrites an existing element a warning is logged and returned with `level === "warn"` and `ok === true`. [0060-overwrite-warning]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_97CD8">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_97CD8";

async signal_97CD8(_, el) {
  const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-element.xml";
  const result = await this.fetchElement("el_97CD8", url);
  if (result.level === "warn" && result.ok === true) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createElement("el_97CD8", `<div>first</div>`);
  this.trigger("signal_97CD8");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Pass options to a fetch call. [0080-pass-options]
HTML
<p>
  NOTE: The documentation/test server does not have
  the ability to modify responses based on 
  request options. Verification is done manually. 
  This automated test is set to always pass
  as a result.
</p>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_7572B">--</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_7572B";

async signal_7572B(_, el) {
  const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-element.xml";
  const options = {
    headers: {
      "x-bitty-test": "data_signal_7572B",
    },
  };
  // await this.fetchElement(key, url, fallback, options);
  //
  // NOTE: Confirming options must be done manually.
  // This test is set to always pass as a result.
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("signal_7572B");
}
Test Trigger
Results

NOTE: The documentation/test server does not have the ability to modify responses based on request options. Verification is done manually. This automated test is set to always pass as a result.

--
Use a POST call to fetch an Element. [0090-with-post]

  NOTE: The documentation/test server does not have

  This automated test is set to always pass
  as a result.
</p>

    method: "POST",
  };
  // await this.fetchElement(key, url, fallback, options);
  //
  // NOTE: Confirming options must be done manually.
  // This test is set to always pass as a result.
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("signal_6B398");
}

Results

NOTE: The documentation/test server does not have the ability to modify responses based on request options. Verification is done manually. This automated test is set to always pass as a result.

Notes
  • TODO: Display parsing HTML Footnote
fetchFragment(key, url, fallback = null, options = {}) [Done]
Description
  • Fetch a document fragment from a URL with a given `key`.
  • The fragment can be used with the related methods: `updateFragment()`, `renderFragment()`, `deleteFragment()`.
Examples
Fetch a fragment from a URL [0010-basic-fetch]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_E47B8">bug</div>
JavaScript
async signal_E47B8(url, el) {
  await this.fetchFragment("el_E47B8", url);
  el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment("el_E47B8").children[1].innerHTML;
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_E47B8");
}

given_signal_E47B8(_, __) {
  const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-fragment.xml";
  this.send(url, "signal_E47B8");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
The fragment is saved to storage if the fetch succeeds. [0040-saved-to-storage]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_0CCFB">bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_0CCFB(_, el) {
  this.loadFragment("el_0CCFB");
  el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment("el_0CCFB").children[1].innerHTML;
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_0CCFB");
}

async given_signal_0CCFB(_, __) {
  const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-fragment.xml";
  await this.fetchFragment("el_0CCFB", url);
  delete this._fragment["el_0CCFB"];
  this.trigger("signal_0CCFB");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Return ok as true if the fetch succeeded. [0050-ok-if-success]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_BE8EB">bug</div>
JavaScript
async signal_BE8EB(url, el) {
  const result = await this.fetchFragment("el_BE8EB", url);
  if (result.ok === true && result.level === "info") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_BE8EB");
}

given_signal_BE8EB(_, __) {
  const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-fragment.xml";
  this.send(url, "signal_BE8EB");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Log and return error if there's a problem with the fetch. [0055-error-if-problem]
HTML
<p>
  NOTE: This test causes expected error messages in the
  console. To avoid noise during the initial load, this
  test is run manually.
</p>
<button data-send="run_signal_C7878">Test Manually</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_C7878">waiting</div>
JavaScript
async signal_C7878(url, el) {
  const result = await this.fetchFragment("el_C7878", url);
  if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


run_signal_C7878(_, __) {
  const url = "/non-exising-page.html";
  this.send(url, "signal_C7878");
}
Test Trigger
Results

NOTE: This test causes expected error messages in the console. To avoid noise during the initial load, this test is run manually.

waiting
Log and return a warning (but still ok === true) if fetch overwrites an existing fragment. [0060-overwrite-warning]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_F9E7D">bug</div>
JavaScript
async signal_F9E7D(url, el) {
  const result = await this.fetchFragment("el_F9E7D", url);
  if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_F9E7D");
}

given_signal_F9E7D(_, __) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createFragment("el_F9E7D", "<div></div>");

}

JavaScript
async signal_11320(payload, el) {
  await this.fetchFragment("el_11320", payload.url, payload.fallback);
  el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment("el_11320").children[0].innerHTML;
}


run_signal_11320(_, __) {
  const fallbackElement = document.createElement("div");
  fallbackElement.innerHTML = "ok";
  const url = "/intentionally-missing-file.html";
  this.send(
    { url: url, fallback: fallbackElement },
    "signal_11320",

}

JavaScript
async signal_80552(payload, el) {

run_signal_80552(_, __) {

Warn if a fallback was used. [0078-warn-if-fallback-used]
HTML
<p>NOTE: This tests causes expected errors in the console. 
  It's run manually to avoid noise on the initial page load
</p>
<button data-send="run_signal_EEB5B">Test Manually</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_EEB5B">waiting</div>
JavaScript
async signal_EEB5B(payload, el) {
  const result = await this.fetchFragment(
    "el_EEB5B",
    payload.url,
    payload.fallback,
  );
  if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  } else {
    el.innerHTML = "bug";
  }
}


run_signal_EEB5B(_, __) {
  const fallbackTemplate = document.createElement("template");
  fallbackTemplate.innerHTML = "<div>ok</div>";
  const url = "/intentionally-missing-file.html";
  this.send(
    { url: url, fallback: fallbackTemplate.content },
    "signal_EEB5B",
  );
}
Test Trigger
Results

NOTE: This tests causes expected errors in the console. It's run manually to avoid noise on the initial page load

waiting
Pass fetch options. [0080-use-options]
HTML
<p>
  NOTE: The serve this site uses ignores extra fetch options. 
  This test shows how to add fetch options 
  but it's not tested here automatically. 
</p>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_6F792"></div>
JavaScript
async signal_6F792(url, el) {
  const options = {
    fetchOptions: {
      headers: {
        "x-bitty-test": "key_signal_6F792",
      },
    },
  };
  await this.fetchFragment("el_6F792", url, null, options);
  el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment("el_6F792").children[1].innerHTML;
}


bittyReady() {
  const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-fragment.xml";
  this.send(url, "signal_6F792");
}
Test Trigger
Results

NOTE: The serve this site uses ignores extra fetch options. This test shows how to add fetch options but it's not tested here automatically.

Make a POST request. [0090-post-call]
HTML
<p>
  NOTE: 
  The serve this site uses does not accept POST
  requests. This test shows how POST requests can be made
  but it's not tested here automatically. 
</p>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_BC346"></div>
JavaScript
async signal_BC346(_, el) {
  const url = "https://www.example.com";
  const options = {
    fetchOptions: {
      method: "POST",
    },
  };
  // The server this site uses doesn't accept
  // POST Requests. This is the line that
  // would make one:
  //
  // await this.fetchFragment(key, url, null, options);
  //
  // Since the server would reject it, it's
  // not tested here.
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("signal_BC346");
}
Test Trigger
Results

NOTE: The serve this site uses does not accept POST requests. This test shows how POST requests can be made but it's not tested here automatically.

fetchJSON(key, url, fallback = null, options = {}) [Done]
Description
  • Fetch a JSON from a URL, add it to `this.json[key]`, and store a copy in localStorage
Examples
Fetching a JSON loads it into the `this.json[key]` collection. [0010-basic-fetch]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_125F4">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "json_signal_125F4";

async signal_125F4(_, el) {
  const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-json.json";
  await this.fetchJSON("el_125F4", url);
//  el.innerHTML = this.json["el_125F4"].status;
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("signal_125F4");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Fallback to a string of JSON if the fetch fails. [0015-fetch-string-fallback]
HTML
<p>NOTE: This tests causes expected errors in the console. 
  It's run manually to avoid noise on the initial page load
</p>
<button data-send="signal_5A151">Test Manually</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_5A151">waiting</div>
JavaScript
#key = "json_signal_5A151";

async signal_5A151(_, el) {
  const url = "/intentionally-missing-file.json";
  const fallback = `{ "status": "ok" }`;
  const result = await this.fetchJSON("el_5A151", url, fallback);
  if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
//    el.innerHTML = this.json["el_5A151"].status;
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results

#key = "json_signal_4F5DF";

async signal_4F5DF(_, el) {

  const fallback = JSON.parse(`{ "status": "ok" }`);
  const result = await this.fetchJSON("el_4F5DF", url, fallback);
  if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {

}

Fallback to a string if there's a parsing issue with the data returned from the URL. [0020-invalid-string-fallback]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_09ED7">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "json_signal_09ED7";

async signal_09ED7(_, el) {
  const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/invalid-json.xjson";
  const fallback = `{ "status": "ok" }`;
  const result = await this.fetchJSON("el_09ED7", url, fallback);
  if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
//    el.innerHTML = this.json["el_09ED7"].status;
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.trigger("signal_09ED7");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Error if a fallback string is used but it doesn't parse. [0021-error-invalid-fallback-string]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_8E522">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "json_signal_8E522";

async signal_8E522(_, el) {
  const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/invalid-json.xjson";
  const fallback = `invalid json`;
  const result = await this.fetchJSON("el_8E522", url, fallback);
  if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.trigger("signal_8E522");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Fallback to a JSON object if the data returned from the URL doesn't parse. [0022-json-fallback]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_A1342">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "json_signal_A1342";

async signal_A1342(_, el) {
  const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/invalid-json.xjson";
  const fallback = JSON.parse(`{ "status": "ok" }`);
  const result = await this.fetchJSON("el_A1342", url, fallback);
  if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.trigger("signal_A1342");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Fallback to a JavaScript object if the data from the URL fails to parse. [0023-jsobj-fallback]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_BE359">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "json_signal_BE359";

async signal_BE359(_, el) {
  const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/invalid-json.xjson";
  const fallback = { status: "ok" };
  const result = await this.fetchJSON("el_BE359", url, fallback);
  if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.trigger("signal_BE359");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Log and return `ok` is `true` when the fetch succeeds. [0030-return-ok]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_F8955">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_F8955");
}

given_signal_F8955(_, __) {
  this.trigger("signal_F8955");
}

async signal_F8955(_, el) {
  const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-json.json";
  const result = await this.fetchJSON("data_signal_F8955", url);
  if (result.ok === true) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Fetched JSONs are stored as well as being added to the collection. [0035-store-on-success]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_DC60C">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "json_signal_DC60C";

signal_DC60C(_, el) {
  this.loadJSON("el_DC60C");
  el.innerHTML = this.json["el_DC60C"].status;
}


async bittyReady() {
  const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-json.json";
  await this.fetchJSON("el_DC60C", url);
  delete this.json["el_DC60C"];
  this.trigger("signal_DC60C");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Log and return error with `ok` is `false` when there's a problem with the fetch call. The full response object is dumped to `console.error()`. The primary strings from the response object are added to the `extraInfo` key in value returned from `fetchJSON()`. [0040-fetch-issue]
HTML
<p>
  NOTE: Fetch errors show up in the console. To avoid
  adding noise to the documentation page this test
  is therefor run manually. 
</p>
<button data-send="signal_5862A">Test Manually</button>
<pre class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_5862A">waiting</pre>
JavaScript
async signal_5862A(_, el) {
  const url = "/file-that-does-not-exist.json";
  const result = await this.fetchJSON("data_signal_5862A", url);
  if (result.ok === false) {
    //el.innerHTML = JSON.stringify(result, null, 2);
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results

NOTE: Fetch errors show up in the console. To avoid adding noise to the documentation page this test is therefor run manually. 

waiting
Log and return error with `ok` is `false` when the JSON can't be parsed. [0050-parse-issue]

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_4734F">bug</div>

#key = "json_signal_78263";

    el.innerHTML = "test passed";

  this.createJSON("el_78263", {});

Pass fetch options with `this.fetchJSON(key, url, options)`. [0080-pass-options]
HTML
<p>
  NOTE: The documentation/test server does not have
  the ability to modify responses based on 
  request options. Verification is done manually. 
  This automated test is set to always pass
  as a result.
</p>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_8C969">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_8C969");
}

given_signal_8C969(_, __) {
  this.trigger("signal_8C969");
}

async signal_8C969(_, el) {
  const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-json.json";
  const options = {
    headers: {
      "x-bitty-test": "data_signal_8C969",
    },
  };
  // await this.fetchJSON("data_signal_8C969", this.#url, options);
  //
  // NOTE: Confirming options must be done manually.
  // This test is set to always pass as a result.
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}
Test Trigger
Results

NOTE: The documentation/test server does not have the ability to modify responses based on request options. Verification is done manually. This automated test is set to always pass as a result.

bug

<p>

  as a result.

given_signal_BDA65(_, __) {

    method: "POST",
  };

NOTE: The documentation/test server does not have the ability to modify responses based on request options. Verification is done manually. This automated test is set to always pass as a result.

bug
fetchSVG(key, url, fallback = null, options = {}) [Done]
Examples
Fetch an SVG from a URL. [0010-basic-fetch]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_74651">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_74651"></div>
JavaScript
#key = "svg_signal_74651";

async signal_74651(_, el) {
  const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-svg.svg";
  await this.fetchSVG("el_74651", url);
  const svg = this.renderSVG("el_74651");
  // el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
  // this.send(svg, "view_signal_74651");
}

view_signal_74651(svg, el) {
  el.replaceWith(svg);
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("signal_74651");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
`fetchSVG()` has a return value object with `.ok === true` when the fetch succeeds. [0030-return-ok]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_A9C93">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_A9C93"></div>
JavaScript
#key = "svg_signal_A9C93";

async signal_A9C93(_, el) {
  const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-svg.svg";
  const result = await this.fetchSVG("el_A9C93", url);
  if (result.ok === true) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
  this.trigger("view_signal_A9C93");
}

view_signal_A9C93(svg, el) {
  el.replaceWith(this.renderSVG("el_A9C93"));
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("signal_A9C93");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Fetched SVGs are added to storage. [0035-add-local-storage]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_B6D8D">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_B6D8D"></div>
JavaScript
#key = "svg_signal_B6D8D";

async signal_B6D8D(_, el) {
  this.loadSVG("el_B6D8D");
  const svg = this.renderSVG("el_B6D8D");
  // el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
  // this.send(svg, "view_signal_B6D8D");
}

view_signal_B6D8D(svg, el) {
  el.replaceWith(svg);
}


async bittyReady() {
  const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-svg.svg";
  await this.fetchSVG("el_B6D8D", url);
  delete this._svg["el_B6D8D"];
  this.trigger("signal_B6D8D");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Return a result with `level === "error"` and `ok === false` if there's an issue with the fetch. [0040-fetch-issue]
HTML
<p>NOTE: This test causes error messages to show up in 
  the console. The test is run manually to prevent
  the noise from showing up on initial page load.</p>
<button data-send="signal_9F02E">Test Manually</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_9F02E">waiting</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_9F02E"></div>
JavaScript
#key = "svg_signal_9F02E";

async signal_9F02E(_, el) {
  const url = "/intentionally-missing-file.svg";
  const result = await this.fetchSVG("el_9F02E", url);
  if (result.level === "error" && result.ok === false) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results

NOTE: This test causes error messages to show up in the console. The test is run manually to prevent the noise from showing up on initial page load.

waiting
If a fetch overwrites an existing SVG a warning is logged and returned with `level === "warn"` and `ok === true`. [0060-overwrite-warning]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_B1D68">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_B1D68"></div>
JavaScript
#key = "svg_signal_B1D68";

async signal_B1D68(_, el) {
  const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-svg.svg";
  const result = await this.fetchSVG("el_B1D68", url);
  if (result.level === "warn" && result.ok === true) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
  this.trigger("view_signal_B1D68");
}

view_signal_B1D68(svg, el) {
  el.replaceWith(this.renderSVG("el_B1D68"));
}


bittyReady() {
  const input = `
vg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
<text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
svg>`;
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createSVG("el_B1D68", input);
  this.trigger("signal_B1D68");
}
Test Trigger
Results

fetchTemplates(url, options = {}) [Done]
Examples
fetch templates uses the same function that loads templates from the page. Just with a URL for the source. [0010-get-templates]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_A1A2F">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_A1A2F"></div>
JavaScript
#key = "key_signal_A1A2F";

async signal_A1A2F(input, el) {
  const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-templates/";
  await this.fetchTemplates(url);
  el.replaceWith(this.renderElement("elementTemplate"));
  this.trigger("view_signal_A1A2F");
}

view_signal_A1A2F(_, el) {
  // el.replaceWith(this.renderSVG("svgTemplate"));
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("signal_A1A2F");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
getGlobalLogLevel()
Description
  • The example test for getGlobalLogLevel changes the global setting. It must be run manually as a result.
Examples
Return the global log level. [0010-return-global-log-level]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_11A34">Test 11A34</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_11A34">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_11A34(_, el) {
  this.setGlobalLogLevel("trace");
  if (this.getGlobalLogLevel() === "trace")  {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  } else {
    el.innerHTML = "test failed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results
waiting
getLocalLogLevel() [Done]
Examples
Return the local log level if it was set explicitly. [0010-return-log-level-string]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_ED724">Test ED724</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_ED724">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_ED724(_, el) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("trace");
  if (this.getLocalLogLevel() === "trace")  {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  } else {
    el.innerHTML = "test failed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_ED724]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Return the global log level as the default if no local log level has been set explicitly. (This test must be run manually since it changes a global setting.) [0020-return-global-default-level]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_F7CAA">Test F7CAA</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_F7CAA">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_F7CAA(_, el) {
  this.setGlobalLogLevel("trace");
  if (this.getLocalLogLevel() === "trace")  {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  } else {
    el.innerHTML = "test failed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results
waiting
info(message)
Examples
json[key] [Done]
Description
  • A collection of JSON objects stored as values for a given `key`.
  • JSONs can be loaded via `createJSON()`, `fetchJSON()`, `loadJSON()` or from script tags with `type="application/json"` and a `data-key` (see TKTKTK section below for more details).
Examples
A basic `createJSON(key, data)` example. See the individual documentation for more details on all the methods. [0010-add-json-example]
HTML
<script type="application/json" data-key="data_signal_E70E0">
  { "status": "ok" }
</script>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_E70E0">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_E70E0");
}

given_signal_E70E0(_, __) {
  this.trigger("signal_E70E0");
}

signal_E70E0(_, el) {
//  el.innerHTML = this.json["data_signal_E70E0"].status;
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
loadElement(key, fallback = null) [Done]
Description
  • Load an element from storage into `this.element[key]`.
  • Use the optional `fallback` if there's no element with the given `key` in storage
Examples
Use `this.loadElement(key)` to load an element from storage into `this.element[key]`. [0010-basic-load]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_23191">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_23191";

signal_23191(_, el) {
  // this.loadElement("el_23191");
  // el.replaceWith(this.renderElement("el_23191"));
}


bittyReady() {
  // this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  // this.deleteElement("el_23191");
  // this.createElement("el_23191", `<div class="test">ok</div>`);
  // delete this._element["el_23191"];
  // this.trigger("signal_23191");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Log and return an `ok` result with `true` if the load succeeds. [0012-return-ok]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_BB0D2">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_BB0D2";

signal_BB0D2(_, el) {
  // const result = this.loadElement("el_BB0D2");
  // if (result.ok === true && result.level === "info") {

}


bittyReady() {
  // this.deleteElement("el_BB0D2");
  // this.createElement("el_BB0D2", `<div>ok</div>`);
  // delete this._element["el_BB0D2"];
  // this.trigger("signal_BB0D2");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Overwrites an existing `this.element[key]` if one exists. Logs and returns a warning of it does so (the `ok` value is still `true`). [0015-overwirtes-existing]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_FFDD5">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_FFDD5";

signal_FFDD5(_, el) {
  const result = this.loadElement("el_FFDD5");
  if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.deleteElement("el_FFDD5");
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createElement("el_FFDD5", `<div>ok</div>`);
  this.trigger("signal_FFDD5");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Strings can be used as fallbacks. [0020-fallback-string]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_B86EE">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_B86EE";

signal_B86EE(_, el) {
  this.loadElement("el_B86EE", `<div class="test">ok</div>`);
  el.replaceWith(this.renderElement("el_B86EE"));
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.deleteElement("el_B86EE");
  this.trigger("signal_B86EE");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Element fallbacks are used. [0030-fallback-element]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_31037">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_31037";

signal_31037(_, el) {
  const newEl = document.createElement("div");
  newEl.classList.add("test");
  newEl.innerHTML = "test passed";
  this.loadElement("el_31037", newEl);
  el.replaceWith(this.renderElement("el_31037"));
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.deleteElement("el_31037");
  this.trigger("signal_31037");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Document Fragment fallbacks can be used for fallbacks. [0031-fallback-fragment]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_BC589">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_BC589";

signal_BC589(_, el) {
  const template = document.createElement("template");
  template.innerHTML = `<div class="test">ok</div>`;
  this.loadElement("el_BC589", template.content);
  el.replaceWith(this.renderElement("el_BC589"));
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.deleteElement("el_BC589");
  this.trigger("signal_BC589");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Only the first element from the root of a fallback document fragment is used. Any siblings are dropped. [0032-fragmnets-truncate]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_77D07">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_77D07";

signal_77D07(_, el) {
  const template = document.createElement("template");
  template.innerHTML = `<div></div><div></div>`;
  const result = this.loadElement("el_77D07", template.content);
  if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.deleteElement("el_77D07");
  this.trigger("signal_77D07");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Fallbacks are added to storage when they are used. [0033-string-stored-fallback]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_9D350">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_9D350";

signal_9D350(_, el) {
  // this.loadElement("el_9D350");
  // el.replaceWith(this.renderElement("el_9D350"));
}


bittyReady() {

  // this.deleteElement("el_9D350");
  // this.loadElement("el_9D350", `<div class="test">ok</div>`);
  // delete this._element["el_9D350"];
  // this.trigger("signal_9D350");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_94D78">bug</div>

#key = "el_signal_94D78";

  const result = this.loadElement("el_signal_94D78");
  if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
