loadFragment(key, fallback = null) [Done]
Examples
Load from storage. [0010-basic-load]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_5D60F">bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_5D60F(_, el) {
  this.loadFragment("el_5D60F");
  el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment("el_5D60F").firstChild.innerHTML;
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_5D60F");
}

given_signal_5D60F(_, __) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createFragment("el_5D60F", "<div>ok</div>");
  delete this._fragment["el_5D60F"];
  this.trigger("signal_5D60F");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Report ok on success. [0015-ok-on-success]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_697D1">bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_697D1(_, el) {
  const result = this.loadFragment("el_697D1");
  if (result.ok === true && result.level === "info") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_697D1");
}

given_signal_697D1(_, __) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.deleteFragment("el_697D1");
  this.createFragment("el_697D1", `<div>ok</div>`);
  delete this._fragment["el_697D1"];
  this.trigger("signal_697D1");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Error if key doesn't exist and there's no fallback. [0017-error-if-no-key-or-fallback]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_7EF03">bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_7EF03(_, el) {
  const result = this.loadFragment("el_7EF03");
  if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_7EF03");
}

given_signal_7EF03(_, __) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.deleteFragment("el_7EF03");
  this.trigger("signal_7EF03");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Warn if overwriting an existing fragment. [0020-overwrite-warning]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_F8382">bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_F8382(_, el) {
  const result = this.loadFragment("el_F8382");
  if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_F8382");
}

given_signal_F8382(_, __) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createFragment("el_F8382", `<div></div>`);
  this.trigger("signal_F8382");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Use string for a fallback. [0030-string-fallback]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_38302">bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_38302(_, el) {
  this.loadFragment("el_38302", `<div></div><div>ok</div>`);
  el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment("el_38302").children[1].innerHTML;
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_38302");
}

given_signal_38302(_, __) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.deleteFragment("el_38302");
  this.trigger("signal_38302");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Use element for a fallback. [0040-element-fallback]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_5A3FB">bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_5A3FB(newElement, el) {
  this.loadFragment("el_5A3FB", newElement);
  el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment("el_5A3FB").children[0].innerHTML;
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_5A3FB");
}

given_signal_5A3FB(_, __) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.deleteFragment("el_5A3FB");
  const newElement = document.createElement("div");
  newElement.innerHTML = "test passed";
  this.send(newElement, "signal_5A3FB");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Use fragment for a fallback. [0050-fragment-fallback]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_119D0">bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_119D0(fallbackFragment, el) {
  this.loadFragment("el_119D0", fallbackFragment);
  el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment("el_119D0").children[1].innerHTML;
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_119D0");
}

given_signal_119D0(_, __) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.deleteFragment("el_119D0");
  const fallbackFragment = document.createDocumentFragment();
  const fallbackElement1 = document.createElement("div");
  const fallbackElement2 = document.createElement("div");
  fallbackElement2.innerHTML = "test passed";
  fallbackFragment.appendChild(fallbackElement1);
  fallbackFragment.appendChild(fallbackElement2);
  this.send(fallbackFragment, "signal_119D0");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Error if a fallback is used but it's invalid. [0055-error-invalid-fallback]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_99F17">bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_99F17(_, el) {
  const result = this.loadFragment("el_99F17", {
    misc: "objects are not valid fallbacks",
  });
  if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_99F17");
}

given_signal_99F17(_, __) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.trigger("signal_99F17");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Fallbacks are stored automatically. [0060-fallbacks-stored]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_D027C">bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_D027C(fallbackFragment, el) {
  this.loadFragment("el_D027C");
  el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment("el_D027C").firstChild.innerHTML;
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_D027C");
}

given_signal_D027C(_, __) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.deleteFragment("el_D027C");
  this.loadFragment("el_D027C", "<div>ok</div>");
  delete this._fragment["el_D027C"];
  this.trigger("signal_D027C");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
loadJSON(key, fallback = null) [Done]
Description

Load a JSON object into the `this.json[key]` collection.

Examples
Use `this.loadJSON(key)` to load a JSON into `this.json[key]`. [0010-json-exists-in-local-storage]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_C89F4">Test C89F4</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_C89F4">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_C89F4(_, el) {
  this.loadJSON("data_C89F4");
  el.innerHTML = this.json.data_C89F4.status;
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  // Create a JSON and then remove it from the
  // collection to simulate it not being 
  // loaded on a page. 
  this.sleep(100);
  this.createJSON("data_C89F4", { status: "test passed" });
  delete this.json["data_C89F4"];
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_C89F4]").click();
}
Results
waiting
`this.loadJSON(key, fallback)` overwrites an existing `this.json[key]` value if it exists. [0015-overwirte-existing-value]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_92D8A">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_92D8A");
}

given_signal_92D8A(_, __) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createJSON(
    "json_signal_92D8A",
    { status: "ok" },
  );
  this.trigger("signal_92D8A");
}

signal_92D8A(_, el) {
  const result = this.loadJSON("json_signal_92D8A");
  if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
    el.innerHTML = this.json["json_signal_92D8A"].status;
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Use `this.loadJSON(key, fallback)` to load a fallback from a string if the key doesn't exist. [0020-fallback-string]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_CEE9A">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_CEE9A");
}

given_signal_CEE9A(_, __) {
  localStorage.removeItem("data_signal_CEE9A");
  this.trigger("signal_CEE9A");
}

signal_CEE9A(_, el) {
  const result = this.loadJSON("data_signal_CEE9A", `{ "status": "ok" }`);
  if (result.ok === true) {
    el.innerHTML = this.json["data_signal_CEE9A"].status;
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Use `this.loadJSON(key, fallback)` to load a fallback from an object if the key doesn't exist. [0030-fallback-object]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_230A6">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_230A6");
}

given_signal_230A6(_, __) {
  localStorage.removeItem("data_signal_230A6");
  this.trigger("signal_230A6");
}

signal_230A6(_, el) {
  const fallback = JSON.parse(`{ "status": "ok" }`);
  const result = this.loadJSON("data_signal_230A6", fallback);
  if (result.ok === true) {
    el.innerHTML = this.json["data_signal_230A6"].status;
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
If a `fallback` string is used it's automatically saved back into localStorage. [0033-string-save-fallback]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_E6EB8">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_E6EB8");
}

given_signal_E6EB8(_, __) {
  const fallback = `{ "status": "ok" }`;
  const result = this.loadJSON("data_signal_E6EB8", fallback);
  this.trigger("signal_E6EB8");
}

signal_E6EB8(_, el) {
  const storage = localStorage.getItem("data_signal_E6EB8");
  el.innerHTML = JSON.parse(storage).data.status;
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
If a `fallback` object is used it's automatically saved back into localStorage. [0035-object-save-fallback]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_A3FE1">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_A3FE1");
}

given_signal_A3FE1(_, __) {
  const fallback = JSON.parse(`{ "status": "ok" }`);
  const result = this.loadJSON("data_signal_A3FE1", fallback);
  this.trigger("signal_A3FE1");
}

signal_A3FE1(_, el) {
  const storage = localStorage.getItem("data_signal_A3FE1");
  el.innerHTML = JSON.parse(storage).data.status;
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Return an error if the `key` doesn't exist and there's no `fallback`. [0040-missing-key-fails]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_194AD">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_194AD");
}

given_signal_194AD(_, __) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  localStorage.removeItem("missing_key_signal_194AD");
  this.trigger("signal_194AD");
}

signal_194AD(_, el) {
  const result = this.loadJSON("missing_key_signal_194AD");
  if (result.ok === false) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Return an error if the `key` doesn't exist and the `fallback` JSON is a string that fails to parse. [0050-error-if-parse-fails]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_F4560">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_F4560");
}

given_signal_F4560(_, __) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.trigger("signal_F4560");
}

signal_F4560(_, el) {
  const result = this.loadJSON("missing_key_signal_F4560", "invalid json");
  if (result.ok === false) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Notes
  • The JSON object is pulled from a `data` key in the localStorage object (i.e. `{ "data": JSON_CONTENT }`.
  • The top level `data` key is what's produces from `this.saveJSON(key)`.
  • The `fallback` value is used if the `key` doesn't exist.
  • Note that `fallback` JSON should not add the top level `data` key. It's added and removed automatically by the `loadJSON` and `saveJSON` functions.
loadSVG(key, fallback = null) [Done]
Examples
Load from storage. [0010-basic-load]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_2D8D5">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_2D8D5"></div>
JavaScript
#key = "svg_signal_2D8D5";

signal_2D8D5(_, el) {
  this.loadSVG("el_2D8D5");
  const svg = this.renderSVG("el_2D8D5");
  // el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
  // this.send(svg, "view_signal_2D8D5");
}

view_signal_2D8D5(svg, el) {
  el.replaceWith(svg);
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_2D8D5");
}

given_signal_2D8D5(_, __) {
  const input = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
<text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>`;
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createSVG("el_2D8D5", input);
  delete this._svg["el_2D8D5"];
  this.trigger("signal_2D8D5");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Report ok on success. [0015-ok-on-success]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_8F8DD">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_8F8DD"></div>
JavaScript
#key = "svg_signal_8F8DD";

signal_8F8DD(_, el) {
  const result = this.loadSVG("el_8F8DD");
  if (result.ok === true && result.level === "info") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
  this.trigger("view_signal_8F8DD");
}

view_signal_8F8DD(svg, el) {
  el.replaceWith(this.renderSVG("el_8F8DD"));
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_8F8DD");
}

given_signal_8F8DD(_, __) {
  const input = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
<text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>`;
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.deleteSVG("el_8F8DD");
  this.createSVG("el_8F8DD", input);
  delete this._svg["el_8F8DD"];
  this.trigger("signal_8F8DD");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Error if key doesn't exist and there's no fallback. [0017-error-if-no-key-or-fallback]

  if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";

  this.trigger("view_signal_2AB87");

<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
<text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>

Results
bug
Use SVG for a fallback. [0050-svg-fallback]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_CEDB1">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_CEDB1"></div>
JavaScript
#key = "svg_signal_CEDB1";

signal_CEDB1(fallback, el) {
  this.loadSVG("el_CEDB1", fallback);
  const svg = this.renderSVG("el_CEDB1");
  // el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
  // this.send(svg, "view_signal_CEDB1");
}

view_signal_CEDB1(svg, el) {
  el.replaceWith(svg);
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_CEDB1");
}

given_signal_CEDB1(_, __) {
  const input = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
<text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>`;
  const template = document.createElement("template");
  template.innerHTML = input;
  const fallback = template.content.querySelector("svg");
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.deleteSVG("el_CEDB1");
  this.send(fallback, "signal_CEDB1");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Error if a fallback is used but it's invalid. [0055-error-invalid-fallback]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_609BD">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "svg_signal_609BD";

signal_609BD(_, el) {
  const result = this.loadSVG("el_609BD", {
    misc: "objects are not valid fallbacks",
  });
  if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.trigger("signal_609BD");
}

<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">

</svg>`;

renderElement(key, substitutions = {}) [Done]
Examples
basic render with no subs [0010-basic-render]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_3EB4D">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_3EB4D";

signal_3EB4D(_, el) {
  el.replaceWith(this.renderElement("el_3EB4D"));
}


bittyReady() {
  this.createElement("el_3EB4D", `<div class="test">ok</div>`);
  this.trigger("signal_3EB4D");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Undefined if the key doesn't exist. (An error is added to the logs) [0015-undefined-if-no-key]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_30B5D">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_30B5D";

bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_30B5D");
}

given_signal_30B5D(_, __) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.trigger("signal_30B5D");
}

signal_30B5D(_, el) {
  this.logs = [];
  if (
    this.renderElement("el_30B5D") === undefined &&
    this.logs[0].level === "error"
  ) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
String text replacements. [0020-text-single-subs]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_3ECFF">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_3ECFF";

signal_3ECFF(_, el) {
  const subs = {
    "TARGET_3ECFF": "ok",
  };
  el.replaceWith(
    this.renderElement("el_3ECFF", subs),
  );
}


bittyReady() {
  this.createElement("el_3ECFF", `<div class="test">TARGET_3ECFF</div>`);
  this.trigger("signal_3ECFF");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Array of string subs [0025-string-array]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_317AA">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_317AA";

signal_317AA(_, el) {
  const subs = {
    "TARGET_317AA": ["o", "k"],
  };
  el.replaceWith(
    this.renderElement("el_317AA", subs),
  );
}


bittyReady() {
  this.createElement("el_317AA", `<div class="test">TARGET_317AA</div>`);
  this.trigger("signal_317AA");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Substitute elements. [0030-element-sub]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_AB8CB">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_AB8CB";

signal_AB8CB(_, el) {
  const replacementEl = document.createElement("div");
  replacementEl.classList.add("test");
  replacementEl.innerHTML = "ok";
  const subs = {
    "TARGET_AB8CB": replacementEl,
  };
  el.replaceWith(
    this.renderElement("el_AB8CB", subs),
  );
}


bittyReady() {
  this.createElement("el_AB8CB", `<div>TARGET_AB8CB</div>`);
  this.trigger("signal_AB8CB");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Use an array of element for substitution. [0035-array-elements]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_F7F0A">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_F7F0A";

signal_F7F0A(_, el) {
  const subs = {
    "TARGET_F7F0A": [
      this.renderElement(`replace1_F7F0A`),
      this.renderElement(`replace2_F7F0A`),
    ],
  };
  el.replaceWith(
    this.renderElement("el_F7F0A", subs),
  );
}


bittyReady() {
  this.createElement("el_F7F0A", `<div>TARGET_F7F0A</div>`);
  this.createElement(`replace1_F7F0A`, `<div class="test">ok</div>`);
  this.createElement(`replace2_F7F0A`, `<div class="test">ok</div>`);
  this.trigger("signal_F7F0A");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Use a document fragment for a substitution. [0040-fragment-single]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_00B6E">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_00B6E";

signal_00B6E(_, el) {
  const subs = {
    "TARGET_00B6E": this.renderFragment(`replacement_00B6E`),
  };
  el.replaceWith(
    this.renderElement("el_00B6E", subs),
  );
}


bittyReady() {
  this.createElement("el_00B6E", `<div>TARGET_00B6E</div>`);
  this.createFragment(
    `replacement_00B6E`,
    `<div class="test">ok</div><div class="test">ok</div>`,
  );
  this.trigger("signal_00B6E");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Use an array of document fragments for a replacement. [0045-fragment-array]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_81AA3">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "el_signal_81AA3";

signal_81AA3(_, el) {
  const subs = {
    "TARGET_81AA3": [
      // this.renderFragment(`replacement1_81AA3`),
      // this.renderFragment(`replacement2_81AA3`),
    ],
  };
  el.replaceWith(
    this.renderElement("el_81AA3", subs),
  );
}


bittyReady() {
  this.createElement("el_81AA3", `<div>TARGET_81AA3</div>`);
  this.createFragment(
    `replacement1_81AA3`,
    `<div class="test">ok</div><div class="test">ok</div>`,
  );
  this.createFragment(
    `replacement2_81AA3`,
    `<div class="test">ok</div><div class="test">ok</div>`,
  );
  this.trigger("signal_81AA3");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
renderFragment(key, substitutions = {}) [Done]
Description
  • Returns a new instance of the requested document fragment for the given `key`.
  • Find and replacement is done with the optional substitutions if they are supplied.
  • The values of substitutions can be: a string, an array of strings, an element, an array of elements, a document fragment, or an array of document fragments.
Examples
Render with no substitutions. [0010-no-subs]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_FC310">bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_FC310(_, el) {
  const fragment = this.renderFragment("el_FC310");
//  el.innerHTML = fragment.children[1].innerHTML;
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_FC310");
}

given_signal_FC310(_, __) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createFragment("el_FC310", `<div></div><div>ok</div>`);
  this.trigger("signal_FC310");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug

renderSVG(key, substitutions = {}) [Done]
Description
Render an SVG with or without substitutions.
Examples
Render with no substitutions. [0010-no-subs]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_57C32">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_57C32"></div>
JavaScript
#key = "svg_signal_57C32";

signal_57C32(_, el) {
  const svg = this.renderSVG("el_57C32");
  // el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
  // this.send(svg, "view_signal_57C32");
 }

view_signal_57C32(svg, el) {
  el.replaceWith(svg);
}


bittyReady() {
  const input = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
<text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>`;
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createSVG("el_57C32", input);
  this.trigger("signal_57C32");
}
Test Trigger
Results

JavaScript
#key = "svg_signal_15988";

signal_15988(subs, el) {
  const svg = this.renderSVG("el_15988", subs);
  // el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").textContent;
  // this.send(svg, "view_signal_15988");
}

view_signal_15988(svg, el) {
  el.replaceWith(svg);
}


bittyReady() {
  const input = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
TARGET_15988
</svg>`;
  const replacementEl = document.createElementNS(
    "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg",
    "text",
  );
  replacementEl.setAttribute("x", "30");
  replacementEl.setAttribute("y", "24");
  replacementEl.setAttribute("font-size", "20");
  replacementEl.setAttribute("text-anchor", "middle");
  replacementEl.setAttribute("fill", "white");
  replacementEl.textContent = "test passed";
  const subs = {
    "TARGET_15988": replacementEl,
  };
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createSVG("el_15988", input);
  this.send(subs, "signal_15988");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Use an array of SVGs for substitutions. [0035-svg-array]

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_697E1">bug</div>

JavaScript
#key = "svg_signal_697E1";

signal_697E1(replacements, el) {
  const subs = {
    "TARGET_697E1": replacements,
  };
  const svg = this.renderSVG("el_697E1", subs);
  const gotString = [
    // svg.querySelectorAll("text")[0].textContent,
    // svg.querySelectorAll("text")[1].textContent,
  ].join("");
  // el.innerHTML = gotString;
  // this.send(svg, "view_signal_697E1");
}

view_signal_697E1(svg, el) {

}


bittyReady() {
  const input = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
TARGET_697E1
</svg>`;
  const replacementEl = document.createElementNS(
    "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg",
    "text",
  );
  replacementEl.setAttribute("x", "24");
  replacementEl.setAttribute("y", "24");
  replacementEl.setAttribute("font-size", "20");
  replacementEl.setAttribute("text-anchor", "middle");
  replacementEl.setAttribute("fill", "white");
  replacementEl.textContent = "o";
  const replacementEl2 = document.createElementNS(
    "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg",
    "text",
  );
  replacementEl2.setAttribute("x", "36");
  replacementEl2.setAttribute("y", "24");
  replacementEl2.setAttribute("font-size", "20");
  replacementEl2.setAttribute("text-anchor", "middle");
  replacementEl2.setAttribute("fill", "white");
  replacementEl2.textContent = "k";
  const replacements = [replacementEl, replacementEl2];
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createSVG("el_697E1", input);
  this.send(replacements, "signal_697E1");
}

saveJSON(key) [Done]
Description
  • Saves the JSON with the given `key` into localStorage
Examples
Save the requested key. [0010-save-valid-key]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_E097F">Test E097F</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_E097F">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_E097F(_, el) {
  this.createJSON(this.key, { status: "bug" });
  this.json[this.key].status = "test passed";
  this.saveJSON(this.key);
  delete this.json[this.key];
  this.loadJSON(this.key);
  el.innerHTML = this.json[this.key].status;
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_E097F]").click();
}
Results
waiting
`saveJSON(key)` logs and returns ok if it's successful [0015-return-ok]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_AF434">Test AF434</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_AF434">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_AF434(_, el) {
  this.key = "data_AF434";
  this.createJSON(this.key, { status: "bug" });
  this.json[this.key].status = "test passed";
  this.saveJSON(this.key);
  const result = this.saveJSON(this.key);
  if (result.ok === true) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_AF434]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Log error if the requested key does not exist. [0020-no-key-error]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_0E048">Test 0E048</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_0E048">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_0E048(_, el) {
  this.setGlobalLogLevel("none")
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none")
  const result = this.saveJSON("no-key-with-this-name");
  if (result.ok === false) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_0E048]").click();
}
Results
waiting
send(payload, signals) [Done]
Description
  • Used to send payloads to trigger signals.
Examples
Send a single signal with `this.api.send(payload, signals)` to a single receiver. [0010-send-a-basic-signal]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_DFC26">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  this.send({ status: "ok" }, "signal_DFC26");
}

signal_DFC26(payload, el) {
  el.innerHTML = payload.status;
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Send a single signal with `this.api.send(payload, signals)` to multiple receivers. [0015-send-signal-with-multiple-receivers]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_138D4">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_138D4">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_138D4">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  this.send({ status: "ok" }, "signal_138D4");
}

signal_138D4(payload, el) {
  el.innerHTML = payload.status;
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
bug
bug
Send multiple signals to a single receiver with `this.api.set(payload, signals)` [0020-send-multiple-signals-to-single-receiver]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_20C90 signal_20C90_2 signal_20C90_3">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  this.send(
    { status: "ok" },
    `signal_20C90 signal_20C90_2 signal_20C90_3`,
  );
}

signal_20C90(payload, el) {
  el.innerHTML = `alfa-${payload.status}`;
}

signal_20C90_2(payload, el) {
  el.innerHTML = `${el.innerHTML}-bravo-${payload.status}`;
}

signal_20C90_3(payload, el) {
  if (payload.status === "ok" && el.innerHTML === "alfa-ok-bravo-ok") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Send multiple signals to multiple receivers with `this.api.send(payload, signals)` [0025-multiple-signals-multiple-receivers]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_CE3AE">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_CE3AE_2">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_CE3AE_3">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  this.send(
    { status: "ok" },
    `signal_CE3AE signal_CE3AE_2 signal_CE3AE_3`,
  );
}

signal_CE3AE(payload, el) {
  el.innerHTML = payload.status;
}

signal_CE3AE_2(payload, el) {
  el.innerHTML = payload.status;
}

signal_CE3AE_3(payload, el) {
  el.innerHTML = payload.status;
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
bug
bug
Send a string. [0027-send-string]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_31AF8">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  this.send("ok", "signal_31AF8");
}

signal_31AF8(payload, el) {
  el.innerHTML = payload;
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Send a signal to a method that doesn't have a corresponding receiver with `this.api.send(payload, signals)` [0030-send-signals-to-method-without-receivers]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_1EF08">bug</div>
JavaScript
#_elIsNull = false;
#_status;

bittyReady() {
  this.send({ status: "ok" }, "signal_1EF08");
}

signal_1EF08(payload, x) {
  this.#_status = payload.status;
  if (x === null) {
    this.#_elIsNull = true;
  }
  this.trigger("verify_signal_1EF08");
}

verify_signal_1EF08(_, el) {
  if (this.#_elIsNull === true) {
    el.innerHTML = this.#_status;
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Signals sent with `this.api.send(payload, signals)` in one bit are received in others. [0040-signals-sent-from-one-bit-are-received-in-others]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_F8EF8_2">bug</div>
JavaScript
async bittyReady() {
  // delay to let other bit initialize
  await this.sleep(100);
  this.send({ status: "ok" }, "signal_F8EF8_2");
}
Extra HTML
<script>
window.ClassF8EF8_2 = class {
  signal_F8EF8_2(payload, el) {
    el.innerHTML = payload.status;
  }
}
</script>

<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassF8EF8_2"></bitty-8-0>
Test Trigger
Results
bug
setCSS(key, value) [Done]
Examples
Set a CSS custom variable property. [0010-set-property]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_E01A5">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("run_signal_E01A5 verify_signal_E01A5");
}

run_signal_E01A5(_, el) {
  this.setCSS("--el-E01A5", "crimson");
}

verify_signal_E01A5(_, el) {
  const gotValue = document
    .documentElement
    .style
    .getPropertyValue("--el-E01A5");
  if (gotValue === "crimson") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
setGlobalLogLevel(level)
Description
  • The example test must be run manually since it changes the global logging value.
Examples
Set the global log level. [0010-set-global-log-level]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_46146">Test 46146</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_46146">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_46146(_, el) {
  this.setGlobalLogLevel("debug");
  if (this.getGlobalLogLevel() === "debug")  {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  } else {
    el.innerHTML = "test failed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results
waiting
setLocalLogLevel(level) [Done]
Examples
Set the log level to any of: global, none, error, warn, info, debug, or trace. [0010-set-level]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_64E6B">Test 64E6B</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_64E6B">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_64E6B(_, el) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("info");
  if (this.getLocalLogLevel() === "info") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  } else {
    el.innerHTML = "test failed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_64E6B]").click();
}
Results
waiting
Set to `warn` and throw error if invalid level is set. [0020-error-on-invalid]
HTML
<p>NOTE: Setting an invalid log level results in 
  the level defaulting to `warn` along with 
  a warning message about the incorrect setting attempt
  being output to the console. This test is run
  manually to avoid that message showing up in the
  console by default.
</p>
<button data-send="signal_04679">Test 04679 Manually</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_04679">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_04679(_, el) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("not-a-valid-level");
  if (this.getLocalLogLevel() === "warn") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  } else {
    el.innerHTML = "test failed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results

NOTE: Setting an invalid log level results in the level defaulting to `warn` along with a warning message about the incorrect setting attempt being output to the console. This test is run manually to avoid that message showing up in the console by default.

waiting
Use `global` to revert to using the global log level. [0030-revert-to-global]
HTML
<p>Test manually since this changes the global log setting</p>
<button data-send="signal_704D8">Test 704D8 Manually</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_704D8">waiting</div>
JavaScript
signal_704D8(_, el) {
  this.setGlobalLogLevel("trace");
  this.setLocalLogLevel("debug");
  this.setLocalLogLevel("global");
  if (this.getLocalLogLevel() === "trace") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  } else {
    el.innerHTML = "test failed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results

Test manually since this changes the global log setting

waiting
sleep(ms) [Done]
Description
  • Provides sleep functionality inside async methods
Examples
Use `this.sleep(ms)` to sleep an `async` method. [0010-sleep-for-time]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_43632">Test 43632</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_43632">waiting</div>
JavaScript
async signal_43632(_, el) {
  this.t1 = performance.now();
  await this.sleep(100);
  this.t2 = performance.now();
  if (this.t2 - this.t1 > 90) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
async bittyReady() {
  this.sleep(100);
  this.qs("[data-send~=signal_43632]").click();
}
Results
waiting
trace(message)
Examples
Notes
trace(message)
trigger(signals) [Done]
Description
  • Used to trigger one or more signals without sending an event or payload to the method.
Examples
Trigger a single signal with a single receiving element. [0010-trigger-a-single-signal]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_C7BB1">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("signal_C7BB1");
}

signal_C7BB1(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Trigger a single signal with multiple receivers. [0015-send-signal-to-multiple-receivers]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_BD30E">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_BD30E">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_BD30E">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("signal_BD30E");
}

signal_BD30E(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
bug
bug
Trigger multiple signals with a single receiver. [0020-multiple-signals-single-reciever]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_86215 signal_86215_2 signal_86215_3">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  this.trigger(`
    signal_86215 
    signal_86215_2
    signal_86215_3
  `);
}

signal_86215(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "alfa";
}

signal_86215_2(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = `${el.innerHTML}-bravo`;
}

signal_86215_3(_, el) {
  if (el.innerHTML === `alfa-bravo`) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Trigger multiple signals with multiple receivers. [0025-multiple-signal-trigger]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_B5F0B">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_B5F0B_2">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_B5F0B_3">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  this.trigger(
    `signal_B5F0B signal_B5F0B_2 signal_B5F0B_3`,
  );
}

signal_B5F0B(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}

signal_B5F0B_2(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}

signal_B5F0B_3(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
bug
bug
Trigger a single signal that doesn't have any corresponding receivers (i.e. elements with `data-receive="SIGNAL"` attributes that match that match a signal in `this.api.trigger(SIGNALS)`). The signal method is triggered once. The second argument passed to it is `null`. [0030-send-to-method-without-a-receiver]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_9C35B">bug</div>
JavaScript
#gotSignal = false;
#gotNull = false;

bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("signal_9C35B");
}

signal_9C35B(_, x) {
  this.#gotSignal = true;
  if (x === null) {
    this.#gotNull = true;
  }
  this.trigger("verify_signal_9C35B");
}

verify_signal_9C35B(_, el) {
  if (this.#gotSignal === true && this.#gotNull === true) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Triggered signals from one bit are received in other bits. [0040-trigger-other-bits]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_B1130_2">bug</div>
JavaScript
async bittyReady() {
  // delay to let other bit initialize
  await this.sleep(100);
  this.trigger("signal_B1130_2");
}
Extra HTML
<script>
window.ClassB1130_2 = class {
  signal_B1130_2(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
</script>

<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassB1130_2"></bitty-8-0>
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Triggers can fire signals that don't have a corresponding element with a `data-receive="SIGNAL"` attribute. When this happens the signal method is called once with `null` for the second argument. [0080-trigger-signal-without-receiver]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_A1F39">bug</div>
JavaScript
#gotNull = false;

bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("run_signal_A1F39");
}

run_signal_A1F39(_, x) {
  if (x === null) {
    this.#gotNull = true;
  }
  this.trigger("verify_signal_A1F39");
}

verify_signal_A1F39(_, el) {
  if (this.#gotNull === true) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
The first argument sent to a signal method form a `this.trigger(SIGNALS)` call is `null`. [0090-null-argument]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_88E0C">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("signal_88E0C");
}

signal_88E0C(ev, el) {
  if (ev === null) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
updateElement(key, content) [Done]
Examples
Update from string. [0010-from-string]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_B814D">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "element_signal_B814D";

signal_B814D(_, el) {
  this.updateElement("el_B814D", `<div class="test">ok</div>`);
  el.replaceWith(this.renderElement("el_B814D"));
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createElement("el_B814D", `<div class="test">bug</div>`);
  this.trigger("signal_B814D");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Log and provide success result. [0020-ok-true-on-success]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_D4BFF">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "element_signal_D4BFF";

signal_D4BFF(_, el) {
  const result = this.updateElement("el_D4BFF", `<div></div>`);
  if (result.ok === true && result.level === "info") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createElement("el_D4BFF", `<div class="test">bug</div>`);
  this.trigger("signal_D4BFF");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Error on fail [0030-ok-false-on-failure]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_BDBD6">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "element_signal_BDBD6";

signal_BDBD6(_, el) {
  const result = this.updateElement("el_BDBD6", {
    key: "not a string, element, or document fragment",
  });
  if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createElement("el_BDBD6", `<div></div>`);
  this.trigger("signal_BDBD6");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
From fragment. [0040-from-fragment]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_66521">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "element_signal_66521";

signal_66521(newElement, el) {
  this.updateElement("el_66521", newElement);
  el.replaceWith(this.renderElement("el_66521"));
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createElement("el_66521", `<div class="test">bug</div>`);
  const fragment = document.createDocumentFragment();
  const element = document.createElement("div");
  element.classList.add("test");
  element.innerHTML = "test passed";
  fragment.appendChild(element);
  this.send(fragment, "signal_66521");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
From element. [0050-from-element]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_45896">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "element_signal_45896";

signal_45896(newElement, el) {
  this.updateElement("el_45896", newElement);
  el.replaceWith(this.renderElement("el_45896"));
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createElement("el_45896", `<div class="test">bug</div>`);
  const element = document.createElement("div");
  element.classList.add("test");
  element.innerHTML = "test passed";
  this.send(element, "signal_45896");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_846F3">bug</div>

#key = "element_signal_846F3";

signal_846F3(_, el) {

  if (result.ok === true && result.level === "info") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.createElement("el_846F3", `<div></div>`);
  this.trigger("signal_846F3");

  if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {

  this.createElement("el_C95B6", `<div></div>`);

#key = "element_signal_E0312";

updateFragment(key, content) [Done]
Description
Updates a fragment or creates it if it doesn't exist.
Examples
Update from string. [0010-from-string]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_5A45F">bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_5A45F(_, el) {
  this.updateFragment("el_5A45F", `<div>x</div><div>ok</div>`);
  el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment("el_5A45F").children[1].innerHTML;
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_5A45F");
}

given_signal_5A45F(_, __) {
  this.trigger("signal_5A45F");
}
Test Trigger
Results

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_FAB09">bug</div>

signal_FAB09(_, el) {

  if (result.ok === true && result.level === "info") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_E5126">bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_E5126(fragment, el) {
  this.updateFragment("el_E5126", fragment);
  el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment("el_E5126").firstChild.innerHTML;
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_E5126");
}

given_signal_E5126(_, __) {
  const fragment = document.createDocumentFragment();
  const element = document.createElement("div");
  element.innerHTML = "ok";
  fragment.appendChild(element);
  this.send(fragment, "signal_E5126");
}

HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_3BC5E">bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_3BC5E(element, el) {
  const result = this.loadFragment("el_3BC5E");
  el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment("el_3BC5E").firstChild.innerHTML;
}

  this.trigger("given_signal_3BC5E");
}

given_signal_3BC5E(_, __) {
  this.deleteFragment("el_3BC5E");
  this.updateFragment("el_3BC5E", `<div>ok</div>`);

  this.trigger("signal_3BC5E");
}

signal_CA48A(_, el) {
  const result = this.updateFragment("el_CA48A", `<div></div>`);

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_00B0F">bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_00B0F(_, el) {
  const result = this.updateFragment("el_00B0F");
  if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_00B0F");
}

given_signal_00B0F(_, __) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.trigger("signal_00B0F");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
No key and no fallback returns error. [0090-no-key-no-value-returns-error]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_6F768">bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_6F768(_, el) {
  const result = this.updateFragment();
  if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("given_signal_6F768");
}

given_signal_6F768(_, __) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.trigger("signal_6F768");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
updateSVG(key, content) [Done]
Description
  • Update an existing SVG.
  • If the SVG doesn't exist in the collection then it gets created
Examples
Add from a string [0010-from-string]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_433FA">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_433FA"></div>
JavaScript
#key = "svg_signal_433FA";

signal_433FA(update, el) {
  this.updateSVG("el_433FA", update);
  const svg = this.renderSVG("el_433FA");
  // el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
  // this.send(svg, "view_signal_433FA");
}

view_signal_433FA(svg, el) {
  el.replaceWith(svg);
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.deleteSVG("el_433FA");
  const input = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
<text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">bug</text>
</svg>`;
  this.createSVG("el_433FA", input);
  const update = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
<text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>`;
  this.send(update, "signal_433FA");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
From another SVG. [0040-from-svg]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_FE475">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_FE475"></div>
JavaScript
#key = "svg_signal_FE475";

signal_FE475(update, el) {
  this.updateSVG("el_FE475", update);
  const svg = this.renderSVG("el_FE475");
  // el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
  // this.send(svg, "view_signal_FE475");
}

view_signal_FE475(svg, el) {
  el.replaceWith(svg);
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.deleteSVG("el_FE475");
  const input = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
<text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">bug</text>
</svg>`;
  this.createSVG("el_FE475", input);
  const template = document.createElement("template");
  template.innerHTML = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
<text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>`;
  const update = template.content.querySelector("svg");
  this.send(update, "signal_FE475");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Store automatically. [0060-store-automatically]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_2EB3B">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_2EB3B"></div>
JavaScript
#key = "svg_signal_2EB3B";

signal_2EB3B(_, el) {
  this.loadSVG("el_2EB3B");
  const svg = this.renderSVG("el_2EB3B");
  // el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").textContent;
  // this.send(svg, "view_signal_2EB3B");
}

view_signal_2EB3B(svg, el) {
  el.replaceWith(svg);
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.deleteSVG("el_2EB3B");
  const input = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
<text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">bug</text>
</svg>`;
  this.createSVG("el_2EB3B", input);
  const update = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
<text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>`;
  this.updateSVG("el_2EB3B", update);
  this.trigger("signal_2EB3B");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Do not warn if overwriting existing key since this is an update. [0070-do-not-warn-on-overwrite]
HTML
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_34AC4">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_34AC4"></div>
JavaScript
#key = "svg_signal_34AC4";

signal_34AC4(update, el) {
  const result = this.updateSVG("el_34AC4", update);
  if (result.ok === true && result.level === "info") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
  this.trigger("view_signal_34AC4");
}

view_signal_34AC4(svg, el) {
  el.replaceWith(this.renderSVG("el_34AC4"));
}


bittyReady() {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("none");
  this.deleteSVG("el_34AC4");
  const input = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
<text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">bug</text>
</svg>`;
  this.createSVG("el_34AC4", input);
  const update = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
<text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>`;
  this.send(update, "signal_34AC4");
}

bug
warn(string)
Description
  • Creates a log entry at the warn level.
  • These example tests output a messages to the console. They're run manually to avoid adding noise to the console during initial load.
Examples
Create a 'warn' log entry from a string. [0010-warn-log]
HTML
<button data-send="signal_5B020">Test 5B020</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_5B020"></div>
JavaScript
signal_5B020(_, el) {
  this.setLocalLogLevel("warn");
  this.warn("This is a warn level log from 5B020");
  el.innerHTML = "Check the console for a warning from 5B020";
}
Test Trigger
Results

Loading Data and Templates from the Page

Overview

  • bitty looks script tags on the page to use for JSON data and templates for Elements, Document Fragments, and SVGs.

Items

Load Element templates from the page.
Examples
Load `type/html` template as an Element template if there's only one element at the top level. [0010-load-element]
HTML
<script type="text/html" data-key="key_signal_72723">
  <div class="test">ok</div>
</script>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_72723">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "key_signal_72723";

signal_72723(update, el) {
//  el.replaceWith(this.renderElement("el_72723"));
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("signal_72723");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Load Document Fragment templates from the page.
Examples
Load `type/html` as a Document Fragment template if there's more than one element at the top level. [0010-fragment-load]
HTML
<script type="text/html" data-key="key_signal_6E2FF">
  <div class="test">ok</div>
  <div class="test">ok</div>
</script>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_6E2FF">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "key_signal_6E2FF";

signal_6E2FF(update, el) {
//  el.replaceWith(this.renderFragment("el_6E2FF").children[1]);
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("signal_6E2FF");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Load JSON data from the page.
Description
  • JSON data can be loaded from script tags on a page.
  • In order for bitty to load data the script tag must have two attributes: `type="application/json` and `data-key="KEY"`.
  • The data is automatically loaded into `this.json[KEY]`.
Examples
Loading JSON data. [0010-json-data-example]
HTML
<script type="application/json" data-key="key_signal_51040">
{ "status": "ok" }
</script>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_51040">bug</div>
JavaScript
#key = "key_signal_51040";

signal_51040(update, el) {
//  el.innerHTML = this.json["el_51040"].status;
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("signal_51040");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
An error is logged and output to the console if JSON in a script tag can't be parsed. [0020-error]
HTML
<!--
NOTE: Invalid JSONs cause error messages to log into
the console. This test is commented out to 
prevent noise on the page during load. Testing
is done manually during development. 
<script type="application/json" data-key="key_signal_1B4CC">
{  invalid json
</script>
-->
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_1B4CC">--</div>
JavaScript
// There's no associated javascript with this example.
// Testing is done manually during development
// to avoid adding noise from expected errors
// to the console.
Test Trigger
Results
--
Load SVG templates from the page.
Examples
Load SVG templates. [0010-svg-load]
HTML
<script type="image/svg" data-key="key_signal_1521B">
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
  <text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>
</script>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_1521B">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_1521B"></div>
JavaScript
#key = "key_signal_1521B";

signal_1521B(input, el) {
  const svg = this.renderSVG("el_1521B");
  // el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
  // this.send(svg, "view_signal_1521B");
}

view_signal_1521B(svg, el) {
  el.replaceWith(svg);
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("signal_1521B");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug

extra event properties and methods

Overview

  • Additional properties and methods that are added to events as they are passed to signal methods.

Items

copyTargetText() [Stub]
Examples
[0010-basic]
HTML
<p>
  NOTE: This example is run manually because it
  uses the operating systems copy/paste system.
  Click inside the input field (which fires
  the 'input' event) and the value will
  be copied to your system copy/paste
  mechanism.
</p>
<input 
  data-send="signal_D0A7B"
  id="el-D0A7B"
  value="value from D0A7B" 
/>
<div class="manual-test">--</div>
JavaScript
async signal_D0A7B(ev, _) {
  await ev.copyTargetText();
}
Test Trigger
Results

NOTE: This example is run manually because it uses the operating systems copy/paste system. Click inside the input field (which fires the 'input' event) and the value will be copied to your system copy/paste mechanism.

--
copyText() [Stub]
Examples
copy the value of the incoming event's sender. [0010-copy-value]
HTML
<p>
  NOTE: This example is run manually because it
  uses the operating systems copy/paste system.
  Click inside the input field (which fires
  the 'input' event) and the value will
  be copied to your system copy/paste
  mechanism.
</p>
<input 
  data-send="signal_D07B2"
  id="el-D07B2"
  value="value from D07B2" 
/>
<div class="manual-test">--</div>
JavaScript
async signal_D07B2(ev, _) {
  await ev.copyText();
}
Test Trigger
Results

NOTE: This example is run manually because it uses the operating systems copy/paste system. Click inside the input field (which fires the 'input' event) and the value will be copied to your system copy/paste mechanism.

--
getData(key, closest = true) [Stub]
Description
  • Gets the value of the sending elements `data-KEY` attribute.
  • TODO: Add test for finding closest
  • TODO: Add test for turning closest off
  • TODO: Add test for undefined
  • TODO: Add test for null
Examples
Get a dataset value from the event's sending element. [0010-get-prop]
HTML
<button 
  id="el-290C3" 
  data-needle="test passed"
  data-send="signal_290C3"
>Run Test</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_290C3">bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_290C3(ev, el) {
  el.innerHTML = ev.getData("needle");
}


bittyReady() {
  document.querySelector("#el-290C3").click();
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug

  id="el-E554E"

  value="2" />

}


bittyReady() {

Test Trigger
Results
bug
sender [Stub]
Description
  • A `.sender` property is added to each incoming event that's sent as the first argument to signal methods.
  • The property is a reference to the element that fired the signal (i.e. the one with the corresponding `data-send` attribute on it.
  • The `.sender` can be the same as the built-in `.target` from the event (e.g. if a button with a `data-send` attribute on it was used).
  • The `.target` element does not have to have a `data-send` attribute on it. For example, a `button` without a `data-send` attribute can be nested inside a `div` that does. In that case, the `button` remains the `.target` but the `div` becomes `.sender`.
  • Events bubble up the DOM. The `.target` element and any number of its ancestors can have `data-send` attributes on them. Each of which will fire a signal when an trigging event occurs.
Examples
A `.sender` property is added to incoming events. [0010-sender-is-target]
HTML
<button 
  data-needle="value_F1196"
  data-send="signal_F1196">
  Run F1196
</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_F1196">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  document.querySelector("[data-send=signal_F1196]").click();
}

signal_F1196(ev, el) {
  if (ev.sender.dataset.needle === "value_F1196") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
The .sender can be an ancestor of the element that initiated the event. [0020-sender-is-ancestor]
HTML
<div data-send="signal_8F115" data-needle="value_8F115">
  <button id="id-8F115">
    Run 8F115
  </button>
</div>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_8F115">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  document.querySelector("#id-8F115").click();
}

signal_8F115(ev, el) {
  if (ev.sender.dataset.needle === "value_8F115") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
Multiple signals with independent senders can be triggered from a single event. [0030-multiple-senders]
HTML
<div data-send="signal_ADBC5_3" data-needle="value_ADBC5-3">
  <div data-send="signal_ADBC5_2" data-needle="value_ADBC5-2">
    <div data-send="signal_ADBC5" data-needle="value_ADBC5">
      <button id="id-ADBC5">
        Run ADBC5
      </button>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_ADBC5">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_ADBC5_2">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_ADBC5_3">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  document.querySelector("#id-ADBC5").click();
}

signal_ADBC5(ev, el) {
  if (ev.sender.dataset.needle === "value_ADBC5") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}

signal_ADBC5_2(ev, el) {
  if (ev.sender.dataset.needle === "value_ADBC5-2") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}

signal_ADBC5_3(ev, el) {
  if (ev.sender.dataset.needle === "value_ADBC5-3") {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
bug
bug

extra element properties and methods

Overview

  • Additional methods that are added to elements as they are passed to signal methods.

Items

copyText() [Stub]
Examples
basic copy of value [0010-basic]
HTML
<p>
  NOTE: This example is run manually because it
  uses the operating systems copy/paste system.
  Click inside the input field (which fires
  the 'input' event) and the value will
  be copied to your system copy/paste
  mechanism.
</p>
<input 
  data-send="signal_C4991"
  id="el-C4991"
  value="value from C4991" 
  data-receive="signal_C4991"
/>
<button data-send="signal_C4991">Run Test</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="verify_signal_C4991">--</div>
JavaScript
async signal_C4991(_, el) {
  await el.copyText();
}
Test Trigger
Results

NOTE: This example is run manually because it uses the operating systems copy/paste system. Click inside the input field (which fires the 'input' event) and the value will be copied to your system copy/paste mechanism.

--
getData(key, closest = true) [Stub]
Examples
Get a dataset value from the event's sending element. [0010-basic]
HTML
<div 
  class="test" 
  data-needle="test passed"
  data-receive="signal_66809"
>bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_66809(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = el.getData("needle");
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("signal_66809");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug

HTML

  data-needle="8.2"
  data-receive="signal_53E81"
>bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_53E81(_, el) {
  if (el.getDataAsFloat("needle") === 8.2) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("signal_53E81");
}

getValue() [Stub]
Examples
Get a dataset value from the event's sending element. [0010-basic]
HTML
<input  
  data-receive="signal_BEA93"
  value="test passed"
/>
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_BEA93">bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_BEA93(_, el) {
  if (el.getValue() === "test passed") {
    this.trigger("verify_signal_BEA93");
  }
}

verify_signal_BEA93(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("signal_BEA93");
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
getValueAsFloat() [Stub]
Examples
Get a dataset value from the event's sending element. [0010-basic]
HTML
<input data-receive="signal_C502D" />
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_C502D">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  document
    .querySelector("[data-receive=signal_C502D]")
    .value = "47.28";
  this.trigger("signal_C502D");
}

signal_C502D(_, el) {
  if (el.getValueAsFloat() === 47.28) {
    this.trigger("verify_signal_C502D");
  }
}

verify_signal_C502D(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
getValueAsInt() [Stub]
Examples
Get a dataset value from the event's sending element. [0010-basic]
HTML
<input data-receive="signal_05EDE" />
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_05EDE">bug</div>
JavaScript
bittyReady() {
  document
    .querySelector("[data-receive=signal_05EDE]")
    .value = 9000;
  this.trigger("signal_05EDE");
}

signal_05EDE(_, el) {
  if (el.getValueAsInt() === 9000) {
    this.trigger("verify_signal_05EDE");
  }
}

verify_signal_05EDE(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = "test passed";
}

isSender() [Stub]
Examples
Check if the current element is also the sender from the incoming event. [0010-basic-check]
HTML
<div 
  id="el-CE356"
  class="test" 
  data-send="signal_CE356"
  data-receive="signal_CE356"
>bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_CE356(_, el) {
  if (el.isSender() === true) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  document.querySelector("#el-CE356").click();
}
Test Trigger
Results
bug
isTarget() [Stub]
Examples
Check if the current element is also the target from the incoming event. [0010-basic-check]
HTML
<div 
  id="el-45333"
  class="test" 
  data-send="signal_45333"
  data-receive="signal_45333"
>bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_45333(_, el) {
  if (el.isTarget() === true) {
    el.innerHTML = "test passed";
  }
}


bittyReady() {
  document.querySelector("#el-45333").click();
}

Results
bug
setData(key, value) [Stub]
Examples
Set data on the current element. [0010-basic]
HTML
<div 
  class="test" 
  data-receive="signal_CE33E verify_signal_CE33E"
>bug</div>
JavaScript
signal_CE33E(_, el) {
  el.setData("updated", "test passed");
  this.trigger("verify_signal_CE33E");
}

verify_signal_CE33E(_, el) {
  el.innerHTML = el.getData("updated");
}


bittyReady() {
  this.trigger("signal_CE33E");
}
